texts.label: text
texts.orig: Installing in a non-production environment will give you time to get familiar with new functionality and complete the setup for reminders and dialogs prior to installing the software in production.
texts.text: Installing in a non-production environment will give you time to get familiar with new functionality and complete the setup for reminders and dialogs prior to installing the software in production.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In programmer mode type, D ^XUP, select the Kernel Installation & Distribution System menu (XPD MAIN), then the Installation option, and then the option LOAD a Distribution.  Enter your directory name and PXRM_2_0_11.KID at the Host File prompt.
texts.text: In programmer mode type, D ^XUP, select the Kernel Installation & Distribution System menu (XPD MAIN), then the Installation option, and then the option LOAD a Distribution.  Enter your directory name and PXRM_2_0_11.KID at the Host File prompt.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: From the Installation menu, you may elect to use the following options:
texts.text: From the Installation menu, you may elect to use the following options:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This option will create a backup message of any routines exported with the patch.  It will NOT back up any other changes such as DDs or templates.
texts.text: This option will create a backup message of any routines exported with the patch.  It will NOT back up any other changes such as DDs or templates.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5 Compare Transport Global to Current System
texts.text: 5 Compare Transport Global to Current System

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This option will allow you to view all changes that will be made when the patch is installed.  It compares all components of the patch (routines, DDs, templates, etc.).
texts.text: This option will allow you to view all changes that will be made when the patch is installed.  It compares all components of the patch (routines, DDs, templates, etc.).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This option will allow you to ensure the integrity of the routines that are in the transport global.  If there are any discrepancies, do not run the Install Package(s) option. Instead, run the Unload a Distribution option to remove the Transport Global from your system.  Retrieve the file again from the anonymous directory (in case there was corruption in FTPing) and Load the Distribution again.  If the problem still exists, log a Remedy ticket and/or call the national Help Desk (1-888-596-HELP) to report the problem.
texts.text: This option will allow you to ensure the integrity of the routines that are in the transport global.  If there are any discrepancies, do not run the Install Package(s) option. Instead, run the Unload a Distribution option to remove the Transport Global from your system.  Retrieve the file again from the anonymous directory (in case there was corruption in FTPing) and Load the Distribution again.  If the problem still exists, log a Remedy ticket and/or call the national Help Desk (1-888-596-HELP) to report the problem.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This option will allow you to view the components of the KIDS build.
texts.text: This option will allow you to view the components of the KIDS build.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: From the Installation menu on the Kernel Installation and Distribution System (KIDS) menu, run the option Install Package(s).  Select the build PXRM*2.0*11 and proceed with the install.  If you have problems with the installation, log a Remedy ticket and/or call the National Help Desk to report the problem.
texts.text: From the Installation menu on the Kernel Installation and Distribution System (KIDS) menu, run the option Install Package(s).  Select the build PXRM*2.0*11 and proceed with the install.  If you have problems with the installation, log a Remedy ticket and/or call the National Help Desk to report the problem.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Test the reminder dialogs in CPRS, using either the exported dialogs or your locally created dialogs. Using point-and-click reminder resolution processing through CPRS GUI, verify the following:
texts.text: Test the reminder dialogs in CPRS, using either the exported dialogs or your locally created dialogs. Using point-and-click reminder resolution processing through CPRS GUI, verify the following:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Correct Progress Note text is posted
texts.text: Correct Progress Note text is posted

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Finding Item gets sent to PCE
texts.text: Finding Item gets sent to PCE

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Check the Clinical Maintenance component display in CPRS after testing dialogs to ensure that all the activities are tested are reflected in the clinical maintenance display.
texts.text: Check the Clinical Maintenance component display in CPRS after testing dialogs to ensure that all the activities are tested are reflected in the clinical maintenance display.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: On the cover sheet, click on the Reminders icon.
texts.text: On the cover sheet, click on the Reminders icon.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Click on reminders in the Reminders box to see details of a reminder
texts.text: Click on reminders in the Reminders box to see details of a reminder

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Open the Notes tab and select New Note. Enter a title and visit information.
texts.text: Open the Notes tab and select New Note. Enter a title and visit information.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Open the Reminders drawer and review the contents.
texts.text: Open the Reminders drawer and review the contents.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Locate the reminder dialog and open it.
texts.text: Locate the reminder dialog and open it.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In the dialog box, check relevant actions.
texts.text: In the dialog box, check relevant actions.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Review the text added to the note to assure its correctness.
texts.text: Review the text added to the note to assure its correctness.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Ensure that the reminder can be satisfied by the individual finding items that were mapped to the reminder terms.
texts.text: Ensure that the reminder can be satisfied by the individual finding items that were mapped to the reminder terms.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Check the Clinical Maintenance component display in Health Summaries and CPRS after the reminder dialog is complete.
texts.text: Check the Clinical Maintenance component display in Health Summaries and CPRS after the reminder dialog is complete.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Repeat steps 5-10 for the Depression Assessment, Iraq & Afghan Post-Deployment Screen, and VA-PTSD SCREENING dialogs.
texts.text: Repeat steps 5-10 for the Depression Assessment, Iraq & Afghan Post-Deployment Screen, and VA-PTSD SCREENING dialogs.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: NOTE: Remember to “refresh” the screen after completing a dialog, if you want to see the updated status immediately. This is especially critical if you’re doing the screening reminder and want to see the follow-up reminder become due because of positive results.
texts.text: NOTE: Remember to “refresh” the screen after completing a dialog, if you want to see the updated status immediately. This is especially critical if you’re doing the screening reminder and want to see the follow-up reminder become due because of positive results.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Before using the VA-Alcohol Use Screen reminder, a TIU/HS Object needs to be created to look for the last occurrence of an AUDIT-C test done on a patient. The TIU/HS Object must be named AUDIT-C for the object to work in the reminder dialog.
texts.text: Before using the VA-Alcohol Use Screen reminder, a TIU/HS Object needs to be created to look for the last occurrence of an AUDIT-C test done on a patient. The TIU/HS Object must be named AUDIT-C for the object to work in the reminder dialog.

table_cells.text: Contains manuals, PowerPoint presentations, and other information about Clinical Reminders
Contains items of interest for all VA mental health clinicians
Contains manuals for Clinical Reminders and Mental Health YS*5.01*85

table_cells.text: Setup after installation by the Reminder Manager or CAC to address local implementation
Approximately one hour (depending partly on the experience level of the manager or CAC)

grid.text: Setup after installation by the Reminder Manager or CAC to address local implementation
Approximately one hour (depending partly on the experience level of the manager or CAC)

table_cells.text: New reminder for branching logic in the new 4th question in the last section of the OEF/OIF reminder. Used for branching logic to indicate in the reminder dialog that an item is already completed or not needed.

VA-CV ELIG W/HF FOR NO SERVICE
VA-CV INELG W/HF FOR SERVICE

These two reminders are designed to aid sites in identifying patients whose combat veteran eligibility data and data collected from the OEF/OIF Screening reminder are not in agreement.
Reminder patient list functionality can be used to identify patients needing reconciliation
New reminder for reporting this monitor.  Excludes non-combat vets from the denominator; requires two visits after discharge separation.

grid.text: New reminder for branching logic in the new 4th question in the last section of the OEF/OIF reminder. Used for branching logic to indicate in the reminder dialog that an item is already completed or not needed.

table_cells.text:
Changes to dialog.
Removed date from term and moved to the health factor for “No alcohol in the past 1 year”
Changes to terms and dialog; verify/report any changes

Multiple changes:
Added Combat Vet to logic
Excluded those who did not serve from denominator
Added cognitive impairment to exclusions
Added done elsewhere to resolutions
Updated dates of health factors for depression/PTSD to 10/1/08
Frequency depends on no retinopathy only

Added veteran status
Added Acute Illness to the dialog
Two new Finding Items were added: VA-COMBAT VET and (FI10) VA-COGNITIVE IMPAIRMENT, and a change in the Cohort and Resolution logic
